text.title: CI Quality Gate Design Output Contract
text.summary: The methodology's output is a single ci-design.md file living next to the CI config. PRs that change CI must also update this file.

list.item: ci-design.md sections: Tier Table (block/warn/nightly), Per-Check Rationale, Time Budget Arithmetic, Escalation Directory, Review Log.
name — check name as it appears in CI.
runtime_p50 — median runtime in minutes over last 30 days.
Total BLOCK runtime under parallelism: sum of critical path.
Slack: minutes of headroom OR over-budget alert.

antipattern.description: CI config introduces a new BLOCK check without a matching ci-design.md entry.
antipattern.reason: Violates r1 + r2. PR must be rejected until ci-design.md is updated.

antipattern.description: Tier table marks a check as BLOCK while the CI runs it `continue-on-error: true`.
antipattern.reason: Configuration drift. Either fix the config to enforce, or move the check to WARN. Lying tables are worse than no tables.

antipattern.description: Budget block shows total > target but no action listed (no demotion, no parallelization plan).
antipattern.reason: The budget arithmetic is only useful if violations trigger an action — otherwise it's documented denial.

list.item: Every CI check appears exactly once in the tier table.
BLOCK checks have rationale + owner + escalation populated.
Runtime numbers updated within last 30 days.
Total BLOCK critical-path runtime <= budget target OR remediation action listed.
Last_reviewed dates within 90 days; older entries flagged.
